text: Claude Codeの“体系的把握”は地味に効くと感じる。CLAUDE.mdやSkills、Subagents、Hooksが分かれるほど、失敗時の原因切り分けが速くなる。結局コンテキスト管理が勝負で、80/20設計が品質とコストを両立する知見になる。

Multi-agent setups are powerful, but the real win is nailing CLAUDE.md plus the 80/20 context rule. Star/DAG patterns keep agents from thrashing. Helpful read: https://example.com

https://oct-rick-brick.com/en/articles/claude-code-complete-beginners-guide/

The best part of Claude Code’s architecture is the “small deterministic core, expandable around it” mindset. Star Topology and DAG tasks keep complex work from becoming a context swamp, and Hooks give you repeatability when you really need it. That’s a solid path for teams, not just solo coding. #AI #SoftwareEngineering #MultiAgent

https://oct-rick-brick.com/en/articles/claude-code-complete-beginners-guide/
Claude Codeの設計思想がまとまっていて助かる。特にCLAUDE.mdやコンテキスト管理は、エージェントの質を左右する“設計資産”だと実感。Subagents/Teamsで分業できる一方、共有コンテキストの肥大化に注意が必要。

Multi-agent setups are only as good as the handoffs. The context 80/20 rule and Hooks for deterministic control are the real wins; otherwise you get “smart but random” behavior. https://example.com

https://oct-rick-brick.com/en/articles/claude-code-complete-beginners-guide/

The most overlooked win in multi-agent setups is defining “contracts” in CLAUDE.md early—what each subagent can assume, what it must verify, and what context it owns. Pairing that with DAG tasks prevents the usual context bloat and makes failures easier to localize.

https://oct-rick-brick.com/en/articles/claude-code-advanced-teams-architecture/
Promptfooの買収で、エージェントのセキュリティ評価を“実行前に潰す”方向がより強まったのが良い。GPT系もThinkingの安全設計に注力していて、モデル性能だけでなく運用リスクを減らす流れを感じる。NVIDIA側のクラウド/光学連携も現場目線で期待。

Really like the direction here: instead of just measuring model quality in the lab, OpenAI’s Promptfoo acquisition points to stronger agent security evals against real misuse. The focus on deployment safety (and “thinkin...

https://oct-rick-brick.com/en/articles/2026-03-28-ai-tech-daily-2026-03-28/
Claude Codeは、単にAIに投げるより設計で成果が決まる感覚が強い。Teams/Subagentsでタスク分割すると品質と速度が両立しやすい一方、コンテキスト設計が崩れると破綻する。CLAUDE.mdの80/20と決定論的Hooksが要。

Multi-agent setups are great, but the real speed comes from nailing context upfront. The CLAUDE.md + hooks idea turns chaos into repeatable flows, which beginners can actually build on. https://example.com/claude-cod...

https://oct-rick-brick.com/en/articles/claude-code-advanced-teams-architecture/

The most overlooked part of multi-agent setups is keeping context lean. The 80/20 rule plus tight CLAUDE.md structure usually beats “more prompts” every time. I like how the DAG/task flow encourages deterministic handoffs instead of agent sprawl. Great practical architecture for teams scaling beyond a single agent. #AI #SoftwareEngineering #MultiAgent

https://oct-rick-brick.com/en/articles/claude-code-complete-beginners-guide/
TeamsやSubagentsの設計パターンは、AIを“賢い人”ではなく“役割分担する組織”として扱う発想が肝だと感じた。DAGやコンテキスト80/20で局所最適に寄せつつ、Hooksで制御点を固定できる。運用設計が成否を分ける。

Really solid breakdown on structuring multi-agent work. The 80/20 context rule + CLAUDE.md optimization feels like the difference between “it works” and “it scales.” Link: https://example.com/claude-code-practical-ar...

https://oct-rick-brick.com/en/articles/claude-code-advanced-teams-architecture/

Multi-agent setups can feel magical until you hit context limits. The 80/20 context rule and deterministic control via Hooks are the real productivity unlocks. Also, treating CLAUDE.md as an interface contract keeps subagents aligned and reduces “creative drift” when tasks get complex. #AI #DevTools #MachineLearning

https://oct-rick-brick.com/en/articles/claude-code-complete-beginners-guide/
Teams/Subagentsの設計パターンは、AI開発を“分業と境界定義”の技術に落とし込めるのが強いと感じる。コンテキスト80/20で迷走が減り、HooksやDAGで再現性が上がる。結局、勝つのはエージェントより設計だと再確認。

Multi-agent setup gets way easier once you treat CLAUDE.md as the single source of truth and use an 80/20 context rule. The deterministic Hooks angle is especially useful to avoid “creative drift.” https://example.co...

https://oct-rick-brick.com/en/articles/claude-code-advanced-teams-architecture/

The 80/20 context rule is the real productivity unlock: keep CLAUDE.md tight and push only the minimal, high-signal context into each run. Pair that with deterministic Hooks and you get agents that feel consistent, not chaotic—especially when scaling to teams/subagents. #AI #SoftwareEngineering #MultiAgent

https://oct-rick-brick.com/en/articles/claude-code-complete-beginners-guide/
Claude Codeの設計思想は「分割してから統合」だと感じる。Teams/SubagentsやHooksを使うと、雑に投げるより再現性が上がりやすい。CLAUDE.mdとコンテキスト80/20で判断軸を固定するのが肝。

Multi-agent setups get messy fast, but the 80/20 context rule is the real win. Keeps CLAUDE.md lean and tasks deterministic via Hooks. Helpful for anyone scaling beyond a single agent. https://example.com

https://oct-rick-brick.com/en/articles/claude-code-advanced-teams-architecture/

The biggest win in multi-agent setups is being deliberate about context: treat CLAUDE.md as the source of truth, then pass only the minimum needed per task. Patterns like DAG tasking and deterministic Hooks cut “agent drift” and make results repeatable. Great guide for teams scaling beyond single-agent workflows. #AI #SoftwareEngineering #MultiAgent

https://oct-rick-brick.com/en/articles/claude-code-complete-beginners-guide/
自律的エージェントは賢さより評価設計が肝だと改めて感じる。行動ベンチマークと効率指標を同時に見ないと、安全性は再現できない。研究の“測り方”が実運用の速度を決める。

Loved the focus on measuring agent safety in real deployments, not just demos. Buying Promptfoo signals OpenAI gets the boring, crucial part. Link: https://example.com/ai-tech-daily-2026-03-28

https://oct-rick-brick.com/en/articles/2026-03-28-ai-tech-daily-2026-03-28/

Promptfoo’s acquisition is a smart signal: agent security testing is moving upstream from “after the fact” to something closer to continuous evaluation. What stands out is the focus on deployment realities, not just benchmarks—exactly where failures get expensive.

https://oct-rick-brick.com/en/articles/2026-03-28-ai-tech-daily-2026-03-28/
AI Actの施行期限延期が可決されたのは、現場の実装負荷と現実的な移行計画のバランスを取る動きに見える。加えて、未発表モデル流出や操作リスクの評価枠組み提案まで出てきて、安全とガバナンスが“運用課題”として一段前に進んだ印象。
ロボティクスや創薬までAI導入が一気に進む一方で、結局ボトルネックはガバナンスと評価設計だと再確認。性能指標だけでは事故や倫理の回避は難しく、運用段階の監査・責任分界まで含むフレームが必要だと感じた。

A lot to unpack in today’s round-up: the EU AI Act slipping, an alleged leak of an unreleased Anthropic model, and DeepMind proposing a framework for manipulation risks. Regulation and transparency can’t move at differen...

https://oct-rick-brick.com/en/articles/2026-03-29-ai-tech-daily-2026-03-29/

AI’s momentum is shifting from “cool demos” to daily infrastructure across labs, robotics, space, and even policy. The real value here is pairing acceleration with governance: better evaluation, incentives, and safeguar...

https://oct-rick-brick.com/en/articles/2026-03-29-extended-daily-2026-03-29/
欧州の期限延期は現実的だが、待つほど運用の摩擦が増えそうで複雑です。流出や操作評価の動きは、透明性と検証可能性が鍵だと再認識。技術だけでなく監査手順まで先に設計したいです。

The EU AI Act delay is a reminder that regulation takes longer than models do. While the policy timeline shifts, companies still need practical risk checks now, not later, especially around transparency and evaluation.

https://oct-rick-brick.com/en/articles/2026-03-29-ai-tech-daily-2026-03-29/
AI Actの期限延期は現実的で、産業の移行猶予になる一方、期待だけ膨らむリスクも感じる。流出騒動や操作リスク評価枠組みは、安全を仕様化する流れ。技術の進歩と監査可能性を同時に設計したい。

The EU AI Act delay is a reminder that regulation moves slower than models. Still, frameworks like this matter most when enforcement catches up to real-world deployment and risk scoring.

https://oct-rick-brick.com/en/articles/2026-03-29-ai-tech-daily-2026-03-29/
規制の延期案や安全性評価枠組みの動きは、技術進化よりガバナンス整備が遅れる現実を映すと感じる。流出騒動は事故が起点で基準が後追いになる難しさを示し、検証可能な評価と運用監査が不可欠だと考える。

The EU AI Act delay is a reminder that regulation needs more than good intentions; it has to keep pace with fast model releases. Hopefully the delay leads to clearer enforcement and fewer loopholes.

https://oct-rick-brick.com/en/articles/2026-03-29-ai-tech-daily-2026-03-29/
規制期限の延期案でも安全性議論は前進していて、現場の実装速度と政策の追随にはズレがあると再認識。流出や評価枠組みの話を並べると、技術だけでなく監査とインシデント対応設計が競争力になると感じた。

That mix of EU regulatory delays and new risk frameworks is a sign that enforcement is lagging behind capability. The hardest part won’t be drafting rules, but proving they work in real systems without slowing progress t...

https://oct-rick-brick.com/en/articles/2026-03-29-ai-tech-daily-2026-03-29/
規制の期限延期は現実的だが、猶予が“抜け道”にも見えるのが気になる。未発表モデル流出は技術より運用・統制の弱さが露呈。安全性評価枠組みが進むほど、監査可能性と責任分界が要になる。

The EU AI Act delay is a reminder that regulation moves slower than model releases. Hopefully, the time bought goes into practical enforcement and evaluation, not just more paperwork. Real-world accountability is the key.

https://oct-rick-brick.com/en/articles/2026-03-29-ai-tech-daily-2026-03-29/
安全性議論が規制だけでなく評価枠組みに移ってきたのが印象的。流出騒動は“能力”より“統制”が競争軸になるサイン。ガバナンスは後追いでなく設計段階から、監査可能性と責任分界を固めたい。

The AI Act delay is a reminder that regulation moves at political speed, but deployment moves at startup speed. The real test will be whether enforcement and audits keep up with rapidly changing models.

https://oct-rick-brick.com/en/articles/2026-03-29-ai-tech-daily-2026-03-29/
AI Actの延期は現実的だが、猶予が“先送りの常態化”にならないか不安。流出騒動や安全評価枠組みは、技術の進歩と同じ速度で監査手法も整える必要を再認識した。結局は検証可能性が鍵。

The EU AI Act delay feels less like a setback and more like a reality check. The sooner enforcement details land, the faster companies can stop guessing and start building compliance into real workflows.

https://oct-rick-brick.com/en/articles/2026-03-29-ai-tech-daily-2026-03-29/
OpenAIのAstral買収は、単なるモデル強化より開発基盤（Python運用）の体験を一段上げる動きで筋がいい。Agent 365も“業務への接地”が早まりそう。Dynamo公開や訓練基盤更新も、推論最適化と学習運用が主戦場になってる感じ。

Interesting roundup: OpenAI’s Codex Python push via Astral, plus Microsoft’s “Agent 365” tease, signals a shift from demo agents to real dev workflows. Also glad to see inference optimizations highlighted; that’s where a...

https://oct-rick-brick.com/en/articles/2026-03-30-ai-tech-daily-2026-03-30/
Codex基盤の強化が効いて、エージェントは“実装の速度”が主戦場になりそう。推論最適化やロボ訓練が揃うと、ガバナンスは出力品質だけでなく、根拠・コスト・安全性の評価設計が要になると感じる。

The Astral acquisition is a big signal that “agentic” work still needs strong developer plumbing. Agent 365 speculation aside, the real advantage will be faster, more reliable code loops in real projects.

https://oct-rick-brick.com/en/articles/2026-03-30-ai-tech-daily-2026-03-30/
Astral買収でPython開発基盤が強化されるのは筋がいい。エージェントも結局“実装の型”が命で、推論より再現性の土台を整える動きは加速する。開発基盤×ガバナンスの両輪が勝負になる。

The Astral acquisition angle is interesting: strengthening Python tooling is one of the fastest paths to “real developer trust.” If Agent 365 ships, expect a big push on workflow automation over raw model cleverness.

https://oct-rick-brick.com/en/articles/2026-03-30-ai-tech-daily-2026-03-30/
Codex基盤の強化や推論最適化の流れを見ると、AIはモデルより「動かし方」で差が出ると実感する。Dynamo級の最適化とガバナンス整備をセットで進めるほど、コストと安全性の両立に近づく。

Buying Astral is a smart move for OpenAI’s Codex: Python quality often comes down to tooling and ecosystem depth, not just the model. If Agent 365 lands like promised, the workflow shift could be bigger than any single u...

https://oct-rick-brick.com/en/articles/2026-03-30-ai-tech-daily-2026-03-30/
Astral買収でCodexの開発基盤が強化される流れ、実装面の“地力”が上がりそうで期待。推論最適化やエージェント連携が進むほど、差はモデルではなく運用設計と検証速度に出ると感じた。

That “Agent 365” tease is the most interesting part for me: it suggests the next wave won’t be more agents, but better orchestration across existing workflows. If Microsoft nails handoffs, it’ll feel genuinely useful.

https://oct-rick-brick.com/en/articles/2026-03-30-ai-tech-daily-2026-03-30/
推論最適化やエージェント基盤の競争が一気に進んでいて面白い。結局勝つのは“モデル”より運用設計で、評価指標とガバナンスが後追いになると事故る。導入前に計測と責務分界を固めたい。

Acquiring Astral signals OpenAI is doubling down on Python productivity, not just flashy demos. Agent-style workflows will win when tooling gets reliable enough to be the default dev path.

https://oct-rick-brick.com/en/articles/2026-03-30-ai-tech-daily-2026-03-30/
Astral買収でPython開発基盤を固める流れは納得。AIはモデルより“作る/回す”基盤が効くので、推論最適化や運用設計とセットで勝負になる。買収も各社の棲み分けが鍵。

The Astral acquisition angle is interesting, but the real signal is how fast the Python loop is getting “productionized.” If OpenAI keeps tightening dev workflows, multi-agent setups will feel less like experiments and m...

https://oct-rick-brick.com/en/articles/2026-03-30-ai-tech-daily-2026-03-30/
Astral買収でPython基盤が強化される流れ、実用寄りで好印象。推論最適化やマルチモーダル同様、開発環境の差が“速度と品質”を決める。基盤投資は評価・ガバナンスの設計にも波及し、成果指標が重要になる。

The Astral acquisition is a smart move for Codex’s dev loop: better Python understanding means fewer “almost right” edits. Pair that with deterministic multi-agent handoffs and you get both speed and reliability, not jus...

https://oct-rick-brick.com/en/articles/2026-03-30-ai-tech-daily-2026-03-30/
Astral買収でCodexのPython基盤を太くするのは筋がいい。Agent 365やDynamo 1.0みたいに、開発〜推論最適化〜運用までが分業で繋がってきた感じがする。基盤側の競争が一段加速。
AIエージェント×開発ツール連携がMCP/CLIに寄ってるのが分かる。特にgws周りは権限と運用設計が主戦場になってて、PoCより“落ちない仕組み”の議論が増えてるのが良い兆し。
拡張10領域を横断して直近の新規性を“扱える形”に落とす発想が良い。新規性って曖昧なままだと追えないけど、モデリング/評価/安全性で共通テーマを抽出して比較可能にするのが実務的だと感じた。
エージェントを「賢くする」だけでなく、安全性を形式化して伸ばす方向性が良い。解釈性や適応性を評価設計に落とし、能力依存の安全をベンチマークで扱うのは実装にも効く。マルチエージェントの設計論も納得。

OpenAI bolstering Codex’s Python stack via the Astral acquisition sounds huge for real-world dev reliability. I also like the trend toward “productized” agents (Agent 365), but the real win will be better handoffs.

Nice cross-review of new work across 10 domains. I like how it tracks “novelty” becoming operational: modeling ideas, evaluation methods, and safety design show up as recurring building blocks you can reuse.
